sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
To start with, you have to tackle the second-longest straight on the calendar, aided by DRS. Top speed is critical here, but so is executing the heavy braking into Turn 1 perfectly. Brake at around the 100m board, down to 2nd gear, and be gentle on turn-in — its very easy to lock up.

Turn 2 is a quick flick to the left — a short shift to reduce traction loss. Turn 3 is a tight right, and your exit here is vital as it leads onto a short straight. Avoid excessive kerb use in Turn 2, or you will destabilise your car and break your momentum for the upcoming DRS straight.

Turn 4 is a 90-degree right-hander; brake at the 100m mark and shift down to 2nd or 3rd gear. Turn 5 comes almost immediately — a left flick where you want to be patient with your throttle. Then you approach Turn 6, a tighter right. Trail brake gently into it and prioritise a clean exit. This entire complex is centred on rhythm and building confidence. Go in too hot, and you will lose all of your momentum in an instant.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
These are medium-speed left-right-left bends. Enter Turn 7 in 4th or 5th gear, lift slightly if needed, and balance the throttle. Turn 8 requires subtle input — you must navigate these S-curves precisely to get them right. The car needs to feel planted; otherwise, you will find yourself either understeering wide or oversteering into a spin.

These two fast flicks complete the S-section. Turn 10 is a quick right with a heavy kerb on the inside. You can take it flat with confidence in time-trial trim. Turn 11 leads into the second DRS zone — get a clean exit here for momentum onto the straight. If you get it wrong and find yourself off the racing line through the esses, your exit speed will suffer enormously.

Use DRS and prepare for the tightest and most technical part of the track — the stadium section. Recharging your ERS battery during a race is common at this point on the track, so manage your ERS usage wisely.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Brake hard into this 90-degree left. It feels wide, but the crowd is right on top of you — the walls narrow your vision. Keep to the inside and take it in 2nd gear. Do not overdo the entry speed, or your exit will be heavily compromised.

This is one of the slowest and most technical sequences on the calendar. Turn 13 is a tight left, immediately followed by the ultra-tight Turn 14 right. Stay in 1st or 2nd gear, short-shift to prevent wheelspin, and use gentle throttle on exit. Turn 15 is a right flick with a tricky bump; it is very easy to lose traction here and spin your rear tyres, so avoid jumping on the throttle too early.

Turn 16 is a wide, double-apex right-hander leading onto the straight. Smooth throttle and early upshifts are crucial. Turn 17 is barely a kink — flat out in qualifying. Open DRS and power down the main straight to start another lap.
